g.text: GPipe vs 1F1B Pipeline Parallelism Memory Comparison

ul.li: Execution: All M forward passes must complete before backward passes start.
Bottleneck: Last stage must wait for all micro-batches to arrive.
Stash Scale: For M=32, device holds 32 sets of activations.
Execution: Once pipeline is full (P steps), each forward is followed by a backward.
Efficiency: Activations are consumed almost immediately after generation.
Stash Scale: For P=4, device holds only 4 sets of activations regardless of M.

div.p: Key Engineering Insight: The 1F1B scheduler decouples activation memory from the number of micro-batches (M). This allows engineers to increase M to amortize optimizer overhead without hitting OOM limits, effectively bypassing the memory wall of traditional pipeline parallelism.

g.title: Linear growth as M micro-batches accumulate activations
Memory is constant as 1 FWD is matched by 1 BWD
Linear growth as M micro-batches accumulate activations
Memory is constant as 1 FWD is matched by 1 BWD
